vol_name: Predicting Event Memory and Trustworthiness in Adolescence
vol_description: To investigate which factors predict event memory and trustworthiness in 12-to 17-year-old adolescents, we tested their memory for events in a video using free recall and multiple-choice recognition tasks. In addition, adolescents completed tasks assessing trustworthiness, theory of mind, decision-making, and self-perception. Demographic information includes age, gender, race and ethnicity, native language, language of instruction in school, country of origin.
